title: ageratina sousae
intro: Ageratina sousae, also known as Hierba del Venado, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, Ageratina sousae has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and even help manage certain types of cancer. Ageratina sousae can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional herbal remedies.
